brief_title: A study in people with moderately to severely active ulcerative colitis to see how well an investigational treatment called PB016 works and how safe it is compared to a licensed treatment called Entyvio®.
official_title: A randomized, double-blind, multicenter phase 3 study in patients with moderately to severely active ulcerative colitis (UC) to compare the efficacy, safety and immunogenicity of PB016 and Entyvio® for the induction and maintenance of clinical response and remission. (UCESIVE)
interventions: Entyvio 300 mg powder for concentrate for solution for infusion | VEDOLIZUMAB | IV INFUSION
outcomes: To demonstrate similarity of effect of induction treatment with (intravenous) IV formulations of PB016 and Entyvio® on clinical response rate at 6 weeks
1. To demonstrate similarity of effect of maintenance treatment with IV formulations of PB016 and Entyvio® on clinical response rate at 52 weeks
2.	To demonstrate similarity of effect of IV PB016 and Entyvio® on partial Mayo score
3.	To demonstrate similarity of effect of IV PB016 and Entyvio® on clinical remission rate
4.	To demonstrate similarity of effect of IV PB016 and Entyvio® on mucosal healing rate
5.	To demonstrate similarity of effect of IV PB016 and Entyvio® on corticosteroid-free remission rate
6.	To demonstrate similarity of effect of IV PB016 and Entyvio® on disease-related biomarkers
7.	To demonstrate similarity of IV PB016 and Entyvio® on pharmacokinetics
8.	To compare the safety profiles of PB016 and Entyvio®
9.	To demonstrate similarity of IV PB016 and Entyvio® on immunogenicity

brief_title: A single dose, randomized, double-blind 2-arm parallel-group study to compare the pharmacokinetics, immunogenicity, and safety for PB016 versus US-licensed Entyvio® after subcutaneous administration in healthy participants
